chunks.text: Japan is one of the most aging societies in the world.
The population aged 65 and above already makes up over 28 percent of the whole.
A long life is something to be celebrated, yet our society faces new challenges.
The workforce is declining, and the pension system has become harder to sustain.
The medical cost of the elderly increases each year, putting pressure on the national budget.
How to sustain welfare is a common challenge for every developed country.
The form of the family has also changed.
More elderly now live apart from their children and grandchildren
and a new problem has appeared: loneliness.
Local communities have begun various efforts to support elderly people who live alone.
and care facilities are trying to make up for the shortage of staff with new technology.
The mutual support between generation will be the foundation of our future society.
The young and the old, each respecting and supporting the other.
This is what creates the true richness of a long - living society.
